questions.stem: The 1956 Dartmouth Conference is significant in AI history because:
options: It produced the first working neural network
It was the first time the term 'Artificial Intelligence' was formally used
It demonstrated the first chess-playing computer
It created the internet
questions.explanation: The 1956 Dartmouth Summer Research Project, organised by John McCarthy and others, formally coined the term 'Artificial Intelligence' and launched AI as a research field.

questions.stem: What was the primary reason for the AI Winters (1970s and 1980s)?
options: Computers were too expensive to build
Scientists lost interest in AI
Initial optimism far exceeded actual progress; computers lacked the data and compute needed
Governments banned AI research
questions.explanation: AI Winters occurred when funding dried up because early AI systems failed to deliver on ambitious promises. The real barriers were insufficient compute, data, and algorithms — not recognised at the time.

questions.stem: AlexNet's victory in the 2012 ImageNet competition is considered a watershed moment because:
options: It was the first computer to beat a human at chess
A deep neural network dramatically outperformed all non-deep methods, proving the power of deep learning at scale
It created the first self-driving car
It was the first algorithm to understand natural language
questions.explanation: AlexNet won ImageNet 2012 by a massive margin using a GPU-trained deep CNN, demonstrating that deep learning + large datasets + GPUs could dramatically surpass classical computer vision. This triggered the modern deep learning era.

questions.stem: The 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need' introduced:
options: Convolutional Neural Networks
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
The Transformer architecture that underpins modern LLMs
Generative Adversarial Networks
questions.explanation: 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017) introduced the Transformer, which replaced RNNs for sequence tasks. All modern LLMs (GPT, BERT, Claude, Gemini) are based on this architecture.

questions.stem: What enabled the deep learning revolution starting around 2012?
options: A new programming language designed for AI
Convergence of big datasets, GPU compute, and improved training algorithms
A breakthrough in understanding of the human brain
The invention of cloud computing
questions.explanation: The deep learning revolution was enabled by three converging factors: massive labelled datasets (ImageNet), affordable GPU compute for parallel training, and improved algorithms like ReLU activations and dropout.

questions.stem: DeepMind's AlphaGo defeating world champion Lee Sedol in 2016 was significant because:
options: Go was considered too complex for computers due to an astronomically large search space
It was the first time a computer played a board game
Go requires physical manipulation of pieces
Lee Sedol was not the strongest player
questions.explanation: Go's search space is ~10^170 — larger than atoms in the universe — making brute-force search impossible. AlphaGo used deep RL and Monte Carlo tree search, showing AI could master domains previously thought beyond reach.
